summary: Angles formed by intersecting lines and by a transversal crossing parallel lines follow a small set of equal/supplementary relationships.

formulas.name: Angles on a line
formulas.expression: \text{sum of angles on a straight line} = 180^\circ

explanation: When two lines intersect, they form two pairs of vertical angles (equal) and adjacent pairs that are supplementary (sum to \(180^\circ\)). When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, eight angles are formed, but there are only two distinct measures: corresponding angles are equal, alternate interior angles are equal, alternate exterior angles are equal, and co-interior (same-side interior) angles are supplementary. A quick way to work these problems: once you know one angle, every other angle is either equal to it or its supplement (\(180^\circ\) minus it).
